---
title: Inventory For A Complex Deployment
execute:
  warning: false
---

This recipe follows a trenched DAS deployment through several metadata updates:

1. A trench with three linear fiber segments starts acquisition on June 1, 2026.
2. The acquisition parameters are corrected after the first inventory was written.
3. In August, the team learns that a fiber break actually occurred in July, so the optical path history is updated retrospectively.
4. After a splice repair, a new optical path segment is added from the repair date forward.

The important pattern is that the patch points at one `data_source_id` for inventory context. The inventory resolves the right historical records for that source at the data time.

## Part 1: Create The Initial Inventory

Start with a simple map: four trench vertices define three straight fiber segments.

```python
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import dascore as dc
from dascore.core import inventory as inv

trench_vertices = np.array(
    [
        [0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
        [100.0, 0.0, 0.0],
        [100.0, 80.0, 0.0],
        [220.0, 80.0, 0.0],
    ]
)
segment_lengths = np.linalg.norm(np.diff(trench_vertices, axis=0), axis=1)
distance_edges = np.concatenate([[0.0], np.cumsum(segment_lengths)])

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3))
ax.plot(trench_vertices[:, 0], trench_vertices[:, 1], marker="o")
for index, (x, y, _) in enumerate(trench_vertices):
    ax.text(x, y + 4, f"P{index}")
ax.set_title("Three-segment trenched fiber")
ax.set_xlabel("x (m)")
ax.set_ylabel("y (m)")
ax.set_aspect("equal", adjustable="box")
```

Now create records for the interrogator, acquisition settings, fiber segments, geometry, coupling, annotations, and the patch-resolvable fiber array.

```python
interrogator = inv.Interrogator(
    resource_id="trench_interrogator",
    manufacturer="Example Instruments",
    model="DAS-1000",
    serial_number="EX-2026-01",
)

acquisition = inv.Acquisition(
    resource_id="trench_acquisition",
    code="RAW",
    location_code="00",
    data_category="DAS",
    data_type="strain",
    data_units="strain",
    interrogator=interrogator,
    start_time="2026-06-01",
    acquisition_sample_rate=500.0,
    gauge_length=10.0,
    pulse_rate=10_000.0,
    spatial_sampling_interval=1.0,
    first_channel_distance=0.0,
)

crs = inv.CoordinateReferenceSystem(
    resource_id="trench_local_crs",
    authority="LOCAL",
    code="trench_local_cartesian",
    axis_order=("x", "y", "z"),
    units="m",
)

fibers = tuple(
    inv.FiberSegment(
        resource_id=f"trench_fiber_{index}",
        name=f"Trench fiber segment {index}",
        optical_length=float(length),
        fiber_type="single_mode",
    )
    for index, length in enumerate(segment_lengths, start=1)
)
geometries = tuple(
    inv.Geometry(
        resource_id=f"trench_geometry_{index}",
        optical_length=float(length),
        geometry_type="linear",
        coordinate_reference_system=crs,
        coordinates=(tuple(start), tuple(stop)),
    )
    for index, (length, start, stop) in enumerate(
        zip(segment_lengths, trench_vertices[:-1], trench_vertices[1:]),
        start=1,
    )
)
coupling = tuple(
    inv.CouplingCondition(
        resource_id=f"trench_coupling_{index}",
        optical_length=float(length),
        coupling_type="buried",
        deployment_type="trench",
        quality="unknown",
    )
    for index, length in enumerate(segment_lengths, start=1)
)
annotations = tuple(
    inv.OpticalPathAnnotation(
        resource_id=f"trench_annotation_{index}",
        distance=(float(start), float(stop)),
        label=f"trench_{index}",
        category="segment",
    )
    for index, (start, stop) in enumerate(
        zip(distance_edges[:-1], distance_edges[1:]),
        start=1,
    )
)

optical_path = inv.OpticalPath(
    resource_id="trench_optical_path",
    name="Trench optical path",
    start_time="2026-06-01",
    optical_length=float(distance_edges[-1]),
    optical_components=fibers,
    geometries=geometries,
    coupling_conditions=coupling,
    annotations=annotations,
).validate()

fiber_array = inv.FiberArray(
    resource_id="trench_array",
    code="TRN",
    name="Three-segment trench array",
    start_time="2026-06-01",
    acquisitions=(acquisition,),
    optical_paths=(optical_path,),
    dims="distance,time",
    tag="trench",
)

network = inv.Network(
    resource_id="trench_network",
    code="XX",
    name="Example trench network",
    description="Example three-segment trench deployment.",
    fiber_arrays=(fiber_array,),
)

inventory = inv.Inventory(
    resource_id="trench_inventory",
    records=(network,),
)

print(inventory.get_record_dicts(record_ids="trench_array")[0])
```

## Part 2: Correct Acquisition Parameters

Later, the team finds the acquisition settings in the first inventory were wrong. The deployment still started on June 1, so replace the acquisition epoch by writing a record with the same `resource_id` and `start_time`.

```python
corrected_acquisition = acquisition.model_copy(
    update={
        "acquisition_sample_rate": 250.0,
        "spatial_sampling_interval": 1.25,
        "comment": "Corrected from field notes after initial inventory creation.",
    }
)

inventory = inventory.put_records(
    records=(corrected_acquisition,),
    author_id="field_team",
)

resolved = inventory.get_records(
    record_ids="trench_array",
    record_types=inv.FiberArray,
    time="2026-06-15",
)[0]

print(resolved.acquisitions[0].acquisition_sample_rate)
print(resolved.acquisitions[0].spatial_sampling_interval)
```

The acquisition history still has one epoch because the corrected record replaced the matching `resource_id` and `start_time`.

```python
history = inventory.get_record_dicts(
    record_ids="trench_acquisition",
    include_history=True,
)

print(len(history))
print(history[0]["acquisition_sample_rate"])
```

## Part 3: Add A Retrospective Fiber Break

In August, the team discovers that the fiber actually broke on July 10 at the end of the second trench segment. The old optical path should remain valid before the break, and a shorter path should apply after the break.

First cap the original open-ended path epoch.

```python
break_time = "2026-07-10"
break_distance = float(distance_edges[2])

capped_path = optical_path.model_copy(
    update={
        "end_time": break_time,
        "comment": "Capped after retrospective fiber-break interpretation.",
    }
)

broken_path = optical_path.select(distance=(0.0, break_distance)).model_copy(
    update={
        "resource_id": "trench_optical_path",
        "start_time": break_time,
        "end_time": "2026-08-20",
        "comment": "Fiber break at the end of trench segment 2.",
    }
)

inventory = inventory.put_records(
    records=(capped_path, broken_path),
    author_id="field_team",
)

before_break = inventory.get_records(
    record_ids="trench_array",
    record_types=inv.FiberArray,
    time="2026-07-01",
)[0]
after_break = inventory.get_records(
    record_ids="trench_array",
    record_types=inv.FiberArray,
    time="2026-08-01",
)[0]

print(before_break.optical_paths[0].optical_length)
print(after_break.optical_paths[0].optical_length)
```

The `FiberArray` record did not change. It still references `trench_optical_path`; inventory time resolution chooses the path epoch.

```python
fiber_array_history = inventory.get_record_dicts(
    record_ids="trench_array",
    include_history=True,
)
path_history = inventory.get_record_dicts(
    record_ids="trench_optical_path",
    include_history=True,
)

print(len(fiber_array_history))
print(len(path_history))
```

## Part 4: Add The Splice Repair Segment

On August 20, the break is repaired by splicing in a replacement segment. Add a new optical path epoch that starts on the repair date and includes the replacement segment.

```python
repair_start = trench_vertices[2]
repair_stop = np.array([235.0, 92.0, 0.0])
repair_length = float(np.linalg.norm(repair_stop - repair_start))
repaired_length = break_distance + repair_length

repair_fiber = inv.FiberSegment(
    resource_id="trench_repair_fiber",
    name="Spliced replacement segment",
    optical_length=repair_length,
    fiber_type="single_mode",
    comment="Installed after July fiber break.",
)
repair_geometry = inv.Geometry(
    resource_id="trench_repair_geometry",
    optical_length=repair_length,
    geometry_type="linear",
    coordinate_reference_system=crs,
    coordinates=(tuple(repair_start), tuple(repair_stop)),
)
repair_coupling = inv.CouplingCondition(
    resource_id="trench_repair_coupling",
    optical_length=repair_length,
    coupling_type="buried",
    deployment_type="trench",
    quality="repaired",
)
repair_annotation = inv.OpticalPathAnnotation(
    resource_id="trench_repair_annotation",
    distance=(break_distance, repaired_length),
    label="splice_repair",
    category="repair",
)

repaired_path = inv.OpticalPath(
    resource_id="trench_optical_path",
    start_time="2026-08-20",
    optical_length=repaired_length,
    optical_components=(*broken_path.optical_components, repair_fiber),
    geometries=(*broken_path.geometries, repair_geometry),
    coupling_conditions=(*broken_path.coupling_conditions, repair_coupling),
    annotations=(*broken_path.annotations, repair_annotation),
).validate()

inventory = inventory.put_records(
    records=(repaired_path,),
    author_id="field_team",
)

repaired = inventory.get_records(
    record_ids="trench_array",
    record_types=inv.FiberArray,
    time="2026-09-01",
)[0]

print(repaired.optical_paths[0].optical_length)
print(repaired.optical_paths[0].annotations[-1].label)
```

## Resolve Metadata For Patch Times

The same patch-level `data_source_id` resolves to different histories depending on data time.

```python
patch = dc.get_example_patch("random_das").update_attrs(
    data_source_id="XX.TRN.00.RAW",
)

june_patch = patch.update_coords(time_min="2026-06-15")
august_patch = patch.update_coords(time_min="2026-08-01")
september_patch = patch.update_coords(time_min="2026-09-01")

june_labels = june_patch.add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("label",),
    on_boundary="ignore",
)
august_labels = august_patch.select(distance=(0, break_distance)).add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("label",),
    on_boundary="ignore",
)
september_labels = september_patch.select(distance=(0, repaired_length)).add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("label",),
    on_boundary="ignore",
)

print(np.unique(june_labels.get_array("label")))
print(np.unique(august_labels.get_array("label")))
print(np.unique(september_labels.get_array("label")))
```
